---
title: "Codebook: Cleaned Data"
format: html
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path

CLEANED_DIR = Path("../cleaned")

# SPF cleaned tables: forecast_individual (wide), forecaster_survey
TABLES = {
    "forecast_individual": {
        "file": "forecast_individual.csv",
        "description": "Individual (microdata) forecasts in wide form: one row per (survey_year, survey_quarter, variable, forecaster_id) with one column per horizon. Horizon columns vary by variable.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "variable", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year (BEA/NIPA definition)."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1–4)."},
            "variable": {"type": "string", "unit": "-", "desc": "Variable code (e.g. CPI, NGDP)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
        }
    },
    "forecaster_survey": {
        "file": "forecaster_survey.csv",
        "description": "One row per (survey_year, survey_quarter, forecaster_id) with industry.",
        "primary_key": ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "forecaster_id"],
        "foreign_keys": {},
        "variables": {
            "survey_year": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey year."},
            "survey_quarter": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "Survey quarter (1–4)."},
            "forecaster_id": {"type": "integer", "unit": "-", "desc": "SPF forecaster identifier."},
            "industry": {"type": "string", "unit": "-", "desc": "Forecaster industry code."},
        }
    },
}


def load_table(filename):
    """Load a CSV table from the cleaned directory."""
    return pd.read_csv(CLEANED_DIR / filename)


def compute_summary(df):
    """Compute summary statistics for a dataframe."""
    summary = []
    for col in df.columns:
        stats = {
            "Variable": col,
            "N": len(df),
            "Missing": df[col].isna().sum(),
            "Unique": df[col].nunique(),
        }
        if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(df[col]):
            stats["Mean"] = f"{df[col].mean():.2f}"
            stats["Std"] = f"{df[col].std():.2f}"
            stats["Min"] = f"{df[col].min():.2f}"
            stats["Max"] = f"{df[col].max():.2f}"
        else:
            stats["Mean"] = "-"
            stats["Std"] = "-"
            stats["Min"] = "-"
            stats["Max"] = "-"
        summary.append(stats)
    return pd.DataFrame(summary)
```

## Table Overview

```{python}
#| echo: false
overview = []
for name, config in TABLES.items():
    filepath = CLEANED_DIR / config["file"]
    if filepath.exists():
        df = load_table(config["file"])
        rows = len(df)
    else:
        rows = "N/A"
    overview.append({
        "Table": f"`{config['file']}`",
        "Description": config["description"],
        "Primary Key": ", ".join(f"`{k}`" for k in config["primary_key"]),
        "Rows": rows,
    })
pd.DataFrame(overview)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

for name, config in TABLES.items():
    filepath = CLEANED_DIR / config["file"]
    
    # Section header
    print(f"\n## {config['file']}\n")
    print(f"{config['description']}\n")
    
    # Keys
    print("### Keys\n")
    print(f"- **Primary Key:** {', '.join(f'`{k}`' for k in config['primary_key'])}")
    if config["foreign_keys"]:
        print("- **Foreign Keys:**")
        for fk, ref in config["foreign_keys"].items():
            print(f"  - `{fk}` → `{ref}.csv`")
    else:
        print("- **Foreign Keys:** None")
    
    # Variables
    print("\n### Variables\n")
    if config["variables"]:
        print("| Variable | Type | Unit | Description |")
        print("|----------|------|------|-------------|")
        for var, meta in config["variables"].items():
            print(f"| `{var}` | {meta['type']} | {meta['unit']} | {meta['desc']} |")
    else:
        print("*TODO: Define variables in TABLES configuration*")
    
    # Summary statistics
    print("\n### Summary Statistics\n")
    if filepath.exists():
        df = load_table(config["file"])
        summary_df = compute_summary(df)
        print(summary_df.to_markdown(index=False))
    else:
        print("*Run the cleaning pipeline first to generate this table*")
    
    print("\n---\n")
```

## By variable: available horizons and forecaster counts

For each variable, this section reports **(E)** which horizons have at least one
non-missing forecast in the data, and **(B)** the number of distinct forecasters
per survey (survey_year, survey_quarter).

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

fi_path = CLEANED_DIR / "forecast_individual.csv"
if not fi_path.exists():
    print("*Run the cleaning pipeline to generate `forecast_individual.csv`.*")
else:
    fi = pd.read_csv(fi_path)
    id_cols = ["survey_year", "survey_quarter", "variable", "forecaster_id"]
    horizon_cols = [c for c in fi.columns if c not in id_cols]

    def is_missing(x):
        if pd.isna(x):
            return True
        if isinstance(x, str):
            s = x.strip().upper()
            return s == "#N/A" or s == "" or s == "NAN"
        return False

    # (E) Available horizons per variable: only horizons with at least one non-missing value
    available_horizons = {}
    for v in fi["variable"].unique():
        sub = fi.loc[fi["variable"] == v, horizon_cols]
        available_horizons[v] = [h for h in horizon_cols if (~sub[h].apply(is_missing)).any()]

    # (B) Distinct forecasters per variable per survey
    forecasters_by_survey = (
        fi.groupby(["variable", "survey_year", "survey_quarter"], as_index=False)["forecaster_id"]
        .nunique()
        .rename(columns={"forecaster_id": "n_forecasters"})
    )

    # Summary table: variable | available horizons | n_surveys | min/mean/max forecasters
    summary_rows = []
    for v in sorted(available_horizons.keys()):
        hlist = available_horizons[v]
        sv = forecasters_by_survey[forecasters_by_survey["variable"] == v]
        n_surveys = len(sv)
        if n_surveys > 0:
            mn, mx = sv["n_forecasters"].min(), sv["n_forecasters"].max()
            mean_f = sv["n_forecasters"].mean()
            summary_rows.append({
                "variable": v,
                "available_horizons": ", ".join(hlist) if hlist else "—",
                "n_surveys": n_surveys,
                "min_forecasters": mn,
                "mean_forecasters": f"{mean_f:.1f}",
                "max_forecasters": mx,
            })
        else:
            summary_rows.append({
                "variable": v,
                "available_horizons": ", ".join(hlist) if hlist else "—",
                "n_surveys": 0,
                "min_forecasters": "—",
                "mean_forecasters": "—",
                "max_forecasters": "—",
            })
    summary_df = pd.DataFrame(summary_rows)
    print("\n### Summary by variable\n")
    print(summary_df.to_markdown(index=False))

    print("\n### Forecaster counts by variable and survey\n")
    for v in sorted(forecasters_by_survey["variable"].unique()):
        sv = forecasters_by_survey[forecasters_by_survey["variable"] == v].copy()
        sv = sv.sort_values(["survey_year", "survey_quarter"])
        print(f"\n#### Variable: {v}\n")
        print(sv.to_markdown(index=False))
        print()
```
